post.content:
YoE: 7 Years
Current workplace: a good enough healthtech startup


I went through 3 gruesome DSA rounds at Uber, for an SSE role.


I was not able to complete the solution for any of them, I mean, I got the approach right for all of them, and some suboptimal solutions [ O(n^2) when O(n) is the ideal one]. But none was close to good IMO (I would reject myself if I were the interviewer).


I wasn't expecting a reply, but I got a postive response from the recruiter, and next rounds are getting scheduled now.


Am I being too hard on myself? Or does Uber have this much tolerance for DSA rounds?
Or are they gonna reject me anyway after conducting the next rounds?
could you share the question?\nsometimes non optimal is acceptable by interviewers too. Good luck for your onsite

Hello there
I got invite for uber onsite. Is it compulsory to go to onsite or they are flexible with remote as well.

Thanks
Any updates here? Please share your interview experience
Depends mainly on HLD rounds and overall debrief
